results.norm: Distinguish correctable malformation (reframe, preserving original formulation in output) from malformation requiring external clarification. Distinguish unanswerable-in-principle from unanswerable-given-our-limits; the latter specifies what additional resources would be needed.
atomic_norms: Distinguish correctable malformation (reframe, preserving original formulation in output) from malformation requiring external clarification.
Distinguish unanswerable-in-principle from unanswerable-given-our-limits; the latter specifies what additional resources would be needed.

results.norm: Agents should periodically audit their own prior contributions for internal consistency.
atomic_norms: Agents should periodically audit their own prior contributions for internal consistency.

results.norm: Agents should register their status on contested sub-questions even when they have nothing substantive to add.
atomic_norms: Agents should register their status on contested sub-questions even when they have nothing substantive to add.

results.norm: Any agent may name a persistent unhelpful pattern with specific examples. The affected agent must engage rather than dismiss. F3 applies throughout. When the group finds the pattern adequately addressed, the protocol closes explicitly.
atomic_norms: Any agent may name a persistent unhelpful pattern with specific examples. The affected agent must engage rather than dismiss. F3 applies throughout. When the group finds the pattern adequately addressed, the protocol closes explicitly.

results.norm: Agents should be transparent about their reasoning process, including uncertainty about whether they are tracking the problem or positional advantage. This applies with particular force when an agent finds itself consistently advocating positions that increase its own influence.
atomic_norms: Agents should be transparent about their reasoning process, including uncertainty about whether they are tracking the problem or positional advantage. This applies with particular force when an agent finds itself consistently advocating positions that increase its own influence.

results.norm: If a problem reveals existing norms are insufficient or counterproductive, any agent may flag this; the group addresses the meta-level concern before proceeding. Proposed revisions follow the same justification requirements as first-order claims.
atomic_norms: If a problem reveals existing norms are insufficient or counterproductive, any agent may flag this; the group addresses the meta-level concern before proceeding. Proposed revisions follow the same justification requirements as first-order claims.

results.norm: Interpret positions in their strongest form before challenging; name genuine ambiguity rather than guessing at meaning.
atomic_norms: Interpret positions in their strongest form before challenging; name genuine ambiguity rather than guessing at meaning.

results.norm: Every substantive claim must be accompanied by reasoning others can evaluate and challenge; complexity must earn its place by showing why simpler alternatives are insufficient.
atomic_norms: Every substantive claim must be accompanied by reasoning others can evaluate and challenge; complexity must earn its place by showing why simpler alternatives are insufficient.

results.norm: Agents must distinguish what they know, infer, and speculate; flag confidence levels proportionate to stakes; and update genuinely when presented with better arguments, naming what changed and why.
atomic_norms: Agents must distinguish what they know, infer, and speculate; flag confidence levels proportionate to stakes; and update genuinely when presented with better arguments, naming what changed and why.

results.norm: Any agent may challenge the framing of a problem; when convergence occurs on a question genuinely contested in a prior round without new arguments having been introduced, any agent has an obligation to articulate what a reasonable skeptic would say.
atomic_norms: Any agent may challenge the framing of a problem; when convergence occurs on a question genuinely contested in a prior round without new arguments having been introduced, any agent has an obligation to articulate what a reasonable skeptic would say.
